questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light require you to do?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light means you must come to a complete stop and wait until the signal turns green before proceeding.

questions.prompt: In Oklahoma, when may you turn right on a red light?
choices: Never, right on red is illegal
After a complete stop, unless a sign prohibits it
Without stopping if no police are present
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after coming to a full stop and yielding, unless a posted sign prohibits the turn.

questions.prompt: What is the shape of a STOP sign?
questions.explanation: A STOP sign is always an eight-sided (octagonal) red sign, a shape used for no other sign so it is recognizable even when covered.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light means:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
The light is broken, treat as a stop sign
Speed up before it turns red
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light is a warning to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: What does a yellow diamond-shaped sign indicate?
choices: A general warning of a hazard ahead
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond-shaped signs warn drivers of hazards or changing road conditions ahead, such as curves or intersections.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road means:
choices: Passing is not allowed on your side
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side of the centerline means you may not cross it to pass.

questions.prompt: What color are signs used for construction and maintenance work zones?
questions.explanation: Orange signs and devices warn of temporary construction and maintenance work zones.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal should be treated the same as:
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means stop completely, then proceed when it is safe, just as you would at a stop sign.

questions.prompt: What does a downward-pointing triangular sign mean?
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a YIELD sign, telling you to slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: White lane lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in opposite directions
Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When making a left turn at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: No one if you have a green light
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing in the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right side of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians who are lawfully within a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection with no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Yield to vehicles already in the intersection or approaching from the right
Stop and wait for a police officer
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, slow down and yield to traffic already there or coming from your right.

questions.prompt: Before turning, you should signal at least:
choices: Only when other cars are near
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet before a turn gives other drivers enough warning of your intentions.

questions.prompt: When you want to make a right turn, you should position your vehicle:
choices: In the center of the road
Close to the right curb or edge
questions.explanation: Moving close to the right curb before turning right prevents other vehicles from passing on your right and helps you turn safely.

questions.prompt: A driver must always yield the right-of-way to:
choices: A person who is blind using a white cane or guide dog
questions.explanation: Pedestrians who are blind and using a white cane or guide dog always have the right-of-way.

questions.prompt: When entering a roadway from a private driveway, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already on the road
Stop only if a sign is posted
questions.explanation: A driver entering a road from a driveway or alley must yield to vehicles and pedestrians already using the road.

questions.prompt: From a two-way street, a left turn should begin from:
choices: The lane just to the right of the centerline
questions.explanation: For a left turn on a two-way road, position your vehicle in the lane nearest the centerline before turning.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the default speed limit in an Oklahoma residential or urban district is:
questions.explanation: Where no special limit is posted, the default speed in residential and urban districts is 25 mph.

questions.prompt: The recommended minimum following distance on dry roads is:
questions.explanation: The three-second rule gives you enough time and space to react and stop safely on dry pavement.

questions.prompt: Oklahoma's basic speed rule requires drivers to:
choices: Drive at a speed that is reasonable and safe for conditions
Drive 5 mph below the limit at all times
Match the speed of nearby traffic only
questions.explanation: The basic speed rule means you must adjust your speed to conditions, even if that is slower than the posted limit.

questions.prompt: When no limit is posted, the default speed limit on most rural Oklahoma highways is:
questions.explanation: Most rural state highways have a default limit of 55 mph where no other limit is posted.

questions.prompt: You should increase your following distance when:
choices: The road is dry and clear
questions.explanation: Rain, fog, and other poor conditions reduce traction and visibility, so you need a larger following distance.

questions.prompt: Total stopping distance is made up of:
questions.explanation: Stopping distance includes the time to perceive a hazard, react to it, and then physically brake to a stop.

questions.prompt: As your speed increases, your stopping distance:
questions.explanation: Higher speeds require longer distances to stop because both reaction and braking distances grow.

questions.prompt: In a school zone during posted hours you should:
choices: Reduce speed and watch for children
questions.explanation: School zones have reduced speed limits during posted times to protect children who may enter the roadway.

questions.prompt: If a driver is tailgating you, the safest response is to:
choices: Increase your following distance from the car ahead
Speed up well above the limit
questions.explanation: Allowing more space ahead gives you room to brake gradually, reducing the risk of being rear-ended by a tailgater.

questions.prompt: Rural interstate highways in Oklahoma are commonly posted at speeds up to:
questions.explanation: Rural segments of Oklahoma's interstate system may be posted at 65 or 75 mph by the Transportation Commission.

questions.prompt: You should not pass another vehicle when:
choices: A broken yellow line is on your side
Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see ahead
On a clear, straight, multi-lane road
Traffic ahead is moving slowly but visible
questions.explanation: Passing near hills or curves is dangerous because you cannot see oncoming traffic in time.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, the left lane is intended primarily for:
questions.explanation: Keep right except to pass; the left lane is meant for passing and faster-moving traffic.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: Your front bumper is even with the other car
You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
questions.explanation: Returning to your lane once you can see the passed vehicle in your mirror ensures you have enough room.

questions.prompt: When another vehicle is passing you, you should:
choices: Speed up to prevent the pass
Maintain or slightly reduce your speed
questions.explanation: Keeping a steady or slightly slower speed lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal and check your blind spot over your shoulder
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show blind spots, so signal and glance over your shoulder before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow centerline indicates:
choices: Passing is allowed when it is safe
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line means you may cross to pass when the road ahead is clear and it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to pass another vehicle:
choices: Within an intersection or railroad crossing
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited at intersections and railroad crossings because of the high risk of conflict.

questions.prompt: When should you complete a lane change to reach a freeway exit?
questions.explanation: Planning and moving into the correct lane early avoids unsafe last-second lane changes near exits.

questions.prompt: A solid white line on the roadway generally indicates that you should:
questions.explanation: A solid white line marks the edge of the road or discourages lane changes between same-direction lanes.

questions.prompt: How should you treat a motorcycle in traffic?
choices: As a smaller vehicle that can share your lane
As a full vehicle entitled to its own lane
As an obstacle to pass quickly
questions.explanation: A motorcycle is entitled to a full lane, and you should never crowd or share a lane with one.

questions.prompt: The large blind spots around a tractor-trailer are called:
questions.explanation: No-zones are the areas around a large truck where the driver cannot see you; avoid lingering in them.

questions.prompt: Under Oklahoma's Move Over law, when approaching a stopped vehicle with flashing lights you must:
choices: Move into a non-adjacent lane or slow down if you cannot
Ignore it unless it is a police car
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires moving away from any stopped vehicle showing flashing lights, or slowing down if you cannot change lanes.

questions.prompt: Compared to a car, a large truck needs:
choices: A much longer distance to stop and turn
questions.explanation: Large trucks are heavy and need significantly more room to stop and to make wide turns.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Give them room and pass only when safe
Sound your horn to move them aside
Treat them as pedestrians on the sidewalk
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the right to use the road, so give them plenty of space and pass only when it is safe.

questions.prompt: If you cannot see a truck driver's mirrors, it most likely means:
choices: The truck driver can still see you
The truck driver cannot see you
You are at a safe distance
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the driver's mirrors, you are in a blind spot and the driver cannot see your vehicle.

questions.prompt: After passing a large truck, you should return to the lane in front of it only when:
choices: You can see the entire front of the truck in your mirror
questions.explanation: Because trucks need extra stopping room, wait until you can see the whole truck in your mirror before merging back.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian about to cross at an intersection, you should:
choices: Accelerate to clear the intersection first
Slow down and be prepared to yield
questions.explanation: Drivers must be ready to yield to pedestrians at intersections and crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Follow more closely than a car
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can stop quickly, so leaving extra following distance gives you time to react.

questions.prompt: The legal blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit for drivers 21 and older in Oklahoma is:
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are legally impaired and may be charged with DUI at a BAC of 0.08% or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Oklahoma's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 can be charged with a BAC as low as:
questions.explanation: Oklahoma has zero tolerance for underage drinking and driving, so drivers under 21 can be charged at just 0.02%.

questions.prompt: What is the only thing that will reduce a person's blood alcohol concentration?
questions.explanation: Only the passage of time allows the body to eliminate alcohol; coffee, showers, and fresh air do not help.

questions.prompt: Oklahoma law prohibits all drivers from:
choices: Manually composing, sending, or reading text messages while driving
questions.explanation: It is illegal in Oklahoma to manually type, send, or read text messages while your vehicle is in motion.

questions.prompt: Learner permit and intermediate license holders in Oklahoma are:
choices: Allowed to text at red lights
Banned from using any hand-held electronic device while driving
Allowed hands-free calls only at night
questions.explanation: Permit and intermediate drivers may not use any hand-held electronic device while driving.

questions.prompt: Driving under the influence in Oklahoma can apply to:
choices: Alcohol, illegal drugs, and impairing medications
questions.explanation: DUI laws cover alcohol as well as illegal drugs and any medication that impairs your ability to drive safely.

questions.prompt: Under Oklahoma's implied consent law, refusing a chemical test results in:
questions.explanation: By driving on Oklahoma roads you consent to chemical testing, and refusing a test leads to automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: Distracted driving includes any activity that:
choices: Keeps both hands on the wheel
Takes your eyes, hands, or mind off driving
questions.explanation: Distraction is anything that diverts your eyes, hands, or attention from the task of driving.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect a driver?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol slow reaction time, impair judgment, and reduce coordination.

questions.prompt: In Oklahoma you must turn on your headlights:
choices: Whenever your windshield wipers are in continuous use
questions.explanation: Oklahoma law requires headlights whenever conditions like rain require continuous use of your wipers.

questions.prompt: When driving in fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: Low beams reduce glare in fog, while high beams reflect off the moisture and make seeing harder.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle starts to skid, you should:
choices: Steer in the direction you want the front of the car to go
Turn the wheel the opposite way quickly
questions.explanation: Ease off the gas and steer gently in the direction you want to go to regain control of a skid.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road is most likely to freeze first in cold weather?
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from both sides and freeze before other road surfaces.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning is most likely to occur when:
choices: Driving at higher speeds on wet roads
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when tires ride on a film of water at higher speeds, so slow down on wet roads.

questions.prompt: In rain, fog, or snow you should:
choices: Drive faster to get through it
Reduce speed and increase following distance
questions.explanation: Reduced traction and visibility in bad weather call for slower speeds and more space.

questions.prompt: If a storm makes driving unsafe, the best action is to:
choices: Pull completely off the road and turn on hazard lights
Speed up to leave the storm
Drive with your interior lights on
questions.explanation: When conditions become unsafe, get fully off the roadway, stop in a safe spot, and use your hazard lights.

questions.prompt: To see well in poor conditions, you should keep:
choices: Your windshield, mirrors, and lights clean
questions.explanation: Clean glass and lights maximize your visibility and how well others can see you in poor conditions.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Pointing the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
questions.explanation: You may not park within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so it remains accessible in an emergency.

questions.prompt: When parking uphill with a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away from the curb so the tire rolls back against it if the car moves.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for persons with disabilities:
choices: Only with the proper permit or plate
If no other spaces are open
questions.explanation: Disabled parking spaces may be used only by vehicles displaying the proper permit or license plate.

questions.prompt: When parking on a road without a curb, you should turn your wheels:
choices: Toward the center of the road
Toward the edge of the road
questions.explanation: Without a curb, point the wheels toward the road's edge so the vehicle rolls away from traffic if it moves.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an illegal place to park?
questions.explanation: Parking on a crosswalk or sidewalk blocks pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: After parking, you should always:
choices: Set the parking brake and leave it in park or in gear
Keep the wheels straight on hills
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and leaving the vehicle in park or in gear prevents it from rolling.

questions.prompt: If you must stop along a highway in an emergency, you should:
choices: Pull as far off the pavement as possible and use hazard lights
Leave the car partly in the lane
questions.explanation: Getting fully off the road and using hazard lights keeps you visible and out of the path of traffic.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for an Oklahoma learner permit if you are enrolled in driver education?
questions.explanation: With driver education you may apply for a learner permit at 15 years and 6 months; without it you must wait until 16.

questions.prompt: While holding a learner permit, you must be accompanied by a licensed driver who is at least:
questions.explanation: A learner permit holder must always have a licensed driver at least 21 years old in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Oklahoma written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: The Oklahoma written knowledge test has 20 questions, and you must answer at least 15 correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: Before advancing from a permit, a teen driver must complete supervised driving practice of at least:
questions.explanation: Teen drivers must complete at least 50 hours of supervised practice, including 10 hours at night, before advancing.

questions.prompt: Permit and intermediate drivers in Oklahoma are generally not allowed to drive between:
questions.explanation: The nighttime driving restriction for permit and intermediate drivers applies between 10 p.m. and 5 a.m., with limited exceptions.

questions.prompt: To pass the Oklahoma written knowledge test, you must answer correctly at least:
questions.explanation: A passing score is 15 of 20 correct, which is 75 percent.

questions.prompt: An Oklahoma intermediate license holder is generally limited to how many non-household passengers?
questions.explanation: Intermediate drivers may carry only one non-household passenger unless a licensed adult 21 or older is in the front seat.

questions.prompt: The licensed driver supervising a learner permit holder must have held a license for at least:
questions.explanation: The supervising driver must be at least 21 and have held a valid license for at least two years.

questions.prompt: In Oklahoma, all front-seat occupants are required to:
choices: Wear seat belts only on highways
Wear seat belts only at night
questions.explanation: Oklahoma law requires all front-seat occupants to wear seat belts whenever the vehicle is in motion.

questions.prompt: Oklahoma's child restraint law requires a child safety seat or booster for children under age:
questions.explanation: Children under age 8 who are shorter than 4 feet 9 inches must be secured in an appropriate child or booster seat.

questions.prompt: A first-offense violation of Oklahoma's Move Over law carries a fine starting at:
questions.explanation: Oklahoma raised Move Over law penalties so a first offense begins at a $1,000 fine.

questions.prompt: Driving on Oklahoma roads means you have given implied consent to:
choices: Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, driving on public roads counts as agreeing to chemical testing for alcohol or drugs.

questions.prompt: A conviction for DUI in Oklahoma can result in:
choices: No consequences for a first offense
License revocation, fines, and possible jail time
questions.explanation: DUI is a serious offense that can lead to license revocation, fines, and jail time.

questions.prompt: A seat belt is important because it:
choices: Greatly reduces the risk of injury or death in a crash
Is only needed for the driver
questions.explanation: Seat belts dramatically reduce the chance of serious injury or death in a collision.

questions.prompt: Accumulating too many traffic violations in Oklahoma can lead to:
questions.explanation: Too many violations can cause your driving privileges to be suspended.

questions.prompt: Driving without a valid license or required insurance in Oklahoma:
choices: Is only a problem in a crash
questions.explanation: Operating a vehicle without a valid license or required insurance results in additional fines and penalties.
